questions.clue: This New England Patriots quarterback won a record seven Super Bowl titles and is widely regarded as the greatest to ever play the position.

citations.title: Tom Brady

questions.clue: This 1980 Namco arcade maze game is famous for its "kill screen": a byte overflow at level 256 garbles the right half of the board into unplayable gibberish.

citations.title: Pac-Man
citations.snippet: Pac-Man, originally titled Puck Man in Japan, is a 1980 maze video game developed and published by Namco for arcades. It was released in Japan on May 22, 1980 and by Midway Manufacturing in North America in August 1980. The player controls

questions.clue: This helical spring toy was invented by American naval engineer Richard T. James in 1943 and first publicly demonstrated at Gimbels department store in Philadelphia.

citations.title: Slinky
citations.snippet: The Slinky is a helical spring toy invented and developed by American naval engineer Richard T. James in 1943 and successfully demonstrated at Gimbels department store in Philadelphia on November 27, 1945.

questions.clue: This American track and field athlete, a Black man competing before Adolf Hitler in Nazi Germany, won four gold medals at the 1936 Berlin Olympic Games.

citations.title: Jesse Owens
citations.snippet: James Cleveland "Jesse" Owens was an American track and field athlete who made history at the 1936 Olympic Games by winning four gold medals, setting individual Olympic records in each event, plus another as a member of the 400 meter relay.

questions.clue: This 1978 Taito arcade game set the template for the fixed-shooter genre by having players repel waves of descending alien attackers.

citations.title: Space Invaders
citations.snippet: Space Invaders is a 1978 shoot 'em up game developed and published by Taito for arcades. Taito released it in Japan in July 1978 and overseas through a partnership with Midway Manufacturing later that year. Space Invaders was the first vide

questions.clue: Nicknamed "Sweetness," this Chicago Bears running back spent 13 NFL seasons with the team; the league's annual award honoring a player's volunteer and community service work is now named after him.

citations.title: Walter Payton

questions.clue: This Portuguese forward became the first man to score at five different World Cups and is the all-time leading goalscorer in men's international football.

citations.title: Cristiano Ronaldo

questions.clue: This coach led the Green Bay Packers to victory in the first two Super Bowls at the conclusion of the 1966 and 1967 seasons; the trophy awarded to the game's winner was later named after him.

citations.title: Vince Lombardi

questions.clue: On March 2, 1962, this NBA center scored 100 points in a single game against the New York Knicks, setting a single-game scoring record that still stands.

citations.title: Wilt Chamberlain

questions.clue: This flame-throwing pitcher struck out a record 5,714 batters and threw a record seven no-hitters over a 27-season career.

citations.title: Nolan Ryan

questions.clue: Nicknamed "the Great One," this Canadian became the only player in NHL history to have his number, 99, retired league-wide.

citations.title: Wayne Gretzky

questions.clue: This 1968 Mexico City gold medalist in the high jump introduced a back-first technique in his lone Olympic appearance that has since seen near-universal adoption among elite competitors in the event.

citations.title: Dick Fosbury

questions.clue: This tennis great won all eleven of his majors at the French Open and Wimbledon, never winning one on hard court.

citations.title: Björn Borg
citations.snippet: Björn Rune Borg is a Swedish former professional tennis player. He was ranked as the world No. 1 in men's singles by the Association of Tennis Professionals (ATP) for 109 weeks. Borg won 66 singles titles during his career, including eleven

questions.clue: This Ethiopian runner won his inaugural gold barefoot on the streets of Rome in 1960 while setting a world record before becoming the first athlete to successfully defend an Olympic marathon title in Tokyo in 1964.

citations.title: Abebe Bikila

questions.clue: Regarded as one of the fastest men ever to play baseball, this center fielder starred in the Negro Leagues and was elected to the Baseball Hall of Fame in 1974.

citations.title: Cool Papa Bell
